title: World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Several elite European national teams enter the expanded 2026 FIFA World Cup with comparable depth, recent major tournament success, and strong qualifying campaigns, keeping implied probabilities tightly clustered near the top. Spain's Euro 2024 triumph and consistent results underpin its narrow lead in trader consensus, while France's back-to-back final appearances sustain its position despite key forward absences. England, Portugal, Argentina, and Brazil follow closely, supported by squad experience and recent form, though injuries to players like Lamine Yamal and others introduce uncertainty that sustains the competitive spread across the field. Home-nation factors for co-hosts provide limited uplift at current pricing.
